summary: The U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz — triggered by the collapse of weekend peace talks — sent Brent crude surging nearly 8% toward $103 a barrel and European gas spiking up to 18%, amplifying inflation fears that are already pressuring central bank policy. Meanwhile, Q1 earnings season kicks off with major banks reporting this week, giving markets a critical fundamental test against the geopolitical noise.
tweet: Hormuz blockade sent Brent crude surging ~8% toward $103 after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed — gold benefits as stagflation fears mount and big bank earnings step into the spotlight. --- SUMMARY: The U.S. blockade of the...
